jokes.text: Space pirates? You know, pirates, but in space.
jokes.setup: Someone expresses confusion about 'space pirates'
jokes.punchline: Fry explains with unnecessary literal obviousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry naive literalism - perfectly in character and economical

jokes.text: Afore I send thee to Davey Jarg's locker!
jokes.setup: Traditional pirate threat about Davy Jones' locker
jokes.punchline: Space pirate says 'Davey Jarg' instead
jokes.explanation: Clever space-pirate wordplay that maintains the rhythm of the original phrase

jokes.text: Hey! Keep it down, you kids! I'm trying to take a nap!
jokes.setup: Dramatic space pirate battle happening
jokes.punchline: Bender treats it like neighborhood kids being loud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender selfishness - treating epic space battle as personal inconvenience

jokes.text: That's not torpedo three! Not at all! Damn you, Fry and Lee... la!
jokes.setup: Fry fires the wrong torpedo in battle
jokes.punchline: Leela's fury builds then deflates as she catches herself mid-curse
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Leela's professionalism overriding her anger mid-sentence

jokes.text: Too late I realize that me children are me only real treasures.
jokes.setup: Pirate captain dying dramatically
jokes.punchline: Cliche deathbed realization delivered in pirate dialect
jokes.explanation: Standard parody of dramatic pirate death scenes, competent but predictable

jokes.text: We can't catch him even if we rub the engine with cheetah blood?
jokes.setup: Leela explains they can't catch Bender going full speed
jokes.punchline: Fry suggests increasingly absurd speed enhancement
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific - 'cheetah blood' is both random and thematically appropriate for speed

jokes.text: I don't know how to say this... But Bender is doomed to drift through space. Forever.
jokes.setup: Leela hesitates to deliver bad news
jokes.punchline: The news is horrifically final and delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from the casual delivery of an eternal doom sentence

jokes.text: He always wanted to drift forever... But through the American Southwest.
jokes.setup: Bender's fate seems to fulfill his dreams
jokes.punchline: But the location is completely wrong - space vs Southwest
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Bender's hobo dreams with perfect ironic contrast

jokes.text: I'm gonna spend eternity alone with barely any swag. Ooh!
jokes.setup: Bender contemplates eternal loneliness
jokes.punchline: His main concern is insufficient treasure, then immediately spots some
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - eternal isolation is bad mainly because of low treasure count

jokes.text: Wow, ostentatious. Except what good is a candelabra without...?
jokes.setup: Bender finds fancy candelabra but questions its usefulness
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of him lighting it with his antenna/exhaust
jokes.explanation: Visual gag utilizing Bender's robot nature - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, cruel fate, to be thusly boned. Ask not for whom the bone bones. It bones for thee.
jokes.setup: Continuing melodramatic soliloquy
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'For Whom the Bell Tolls' using Bender's catchphrase 'boned'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary parody that perfectly integrates Bender's slang with Hemingway/Donne. Highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: The only thing that keeps me sane... Is the thought that I have all eternity in which to perfect my art.
jokes.setup: Bender finds solace in artistic pursuits
jokes.punchline: Cut to him apparently carving crude figures or practicing simple skills
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for visual gag of Bender's 'art' being crude or simple

jokes.text: Now, when I'm found in a million years, people will know what the score was.
jokes.setup: Bender has been working on his 'art'
jokes.punchline: His eternal artistic legacy is just keeping score of a sports game
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of artistic pretensions - his eternal legacy is just sports scores

jokes.text: It could hurt slightly.
jokes.setup: Asteroid could go through Bender's skull
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of potential brain damage
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor - death described as slight discomfort

jokes.text: Holy frijoles. All bow before the great Metal Lord.
jokes.setup: Aliens worship Bender as god
jokes.punchline: 'Holy beans' as alien sacred oath, combined with 'Metal Lord' title
jokes.explanation: Mild bilingual pun with appropriate religious awe language

jokes.text: Those peewees think I'm God, huh? Hard to blame them.
jokes.setup: Tiny aliens worship Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately accepts this as reasonable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ego - of course they'd worship him, who wouldn't?

jokes.text: It means 'He who really loves the Metal Lord.' Listen here, time for a religious donation. Hand over your wallet!
jokes.setup: Alien explains his name shows devotion
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately tries to monetize worship
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender greed - can't even pretend to be a benevolent god for five seconds

jokes.text: But we are a poor and simple folk. Poor? Oh, crap!
jokes.setup: Aliens explain they're poor and humble
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate disappointment at no profit potential
jokes.explanation: Bender's disappointment at worshippers having no money is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Gag unto me with a spoon. It's time I laid down a few commandments.
jokes.setup: Bender watches alien family bonding
jokes.punchline: Valley girl 'gag me with a spoon' mixed with biblical 'unto' language
jokes.explanation: Clever mashup of valley speak and biblical language - very quotable

jokes.text: Behold, the one commandment: Make it a double!
jokes.setup: Grand buildup to divine commandments
jokes.punchline: Single commandment is just a bar order
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation - all of divine wisdom reduced to Bender's drinking preference

jokes.text: Hooray! People are paying attention to me.
jokes.setup: Others trying to cheer up grieving Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry's shallow excitement about getting attention rather than comfort
jokes.explanation: Good Fry characterization - missing the point and focusing on superficial benefits

jokes.text: His name is Helper. He is eager to be your new best friend. Hello, chum. Miss your buddy?
jokes.setup: Crew tries to cheer up Fry with replacement friend
jokes.punchline: Helper's forced enthusiasm and immediate invasive question
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward replacement friend trope - functional but predictable

jokes.text: No. I just thought you would enjoy that for a moment.
jokes.setup: Helper claims Bender is back
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he was lying for Fry's brief happiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected - Helper's casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Yes, tonight is kind of special. Did you rip off your arm as a joke?
jokes.setup: Malachi mentions tonight being special
jokes.punchline: Bender casually asks about horrific injury like it's a prank
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Bender's casual attitude toward his followers' suffering

jokes.text: Nay. Like most of your followers, I was maimed building the great brewery.
jokes.setup: Explaining his missing arm
jokes.punchline: Mass casualties from building Bender's beer facility
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about the costs of Bender's hedonistic demands on his worshippers

jokes.text: And, of course, the liquor industry attracted organized crime.
jokes.setup: Already mentioned industrial accidents and toxic fumes
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact addition of organized crime problems
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of problems caused by Bender's brewery, delivered casually

jokes.text: This looks like a job for God.
jokes.setup: Child in mortal danger needs divine intervention
jokes.punchline: Bender treats it like a superhero catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Nice parody of Superman's catchphrase applied to actual godhood

jokes.text: That was a practice miracle. Who's next?
jokes.setup: Bender accidentally causes destruction while trying to help
jokes.punchline: Dismisses disaster as 'practice' and moves on casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender callousness - treating catastrophic failure as minor rehearsal

jokes.text: When you're writing the Bible, you might want to omit that last miracle.
jokes.setup: Bender's 'miracle' apparently caused more damage
jokes.punchline: Suggestion to edit out the embarrassing parts from scripture
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about biblical editing and divine PR management

jokes.text: We could join together in prayer. But is there anything useful we can do? No.
jokes.setup: Priest offers prayer as solution
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits prayer has no practical value
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious satire - priest's honest admission of prayer's ineffectiveness

jokes.text: I am your friend, Bonder. Bonder? Is it really you? Yes. I am fine. Give the gypsy $10.
jokes.setup: Psychic claims to channel Bender
jokes.punchline: Gets name wrong and immediately asks for money
jokes.explanation: Standard fake psychic tropes - wrong name and immediate payment request

jokes.text: You want false hope or not? Only if there's no real hope.
jokes.setup: Fry calls out the psychic's fraud
jokes.punchline: Honest transaction offer: fake hope as backup plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - honest admission of selling false hope as legitimate service

jokes.text: They'd let me use it to find Bender? What am I, psychic? I mean, yes. I'm sure they will. Yes.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if monks would help
jokes.punchline: Priest catches himself giving honest 'I don't know' then switches to confident fake answer
jokes.explanation: Nice beat where priest realizes his honest uncertainty undermines his authority

jokes.text: How am I supposed to hear prayers coming out of my ass?
jokes.setup: Followers on Bender's back complain their prayers go unheard
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude but anatomically accurate point about prayer logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crude Bender logic applied to theological problem

jokes.text: What part of nay doesn't thou understand?
jokes.setup: Malachi continues pleading after Bender refuses
jokes.punchline: Modern dismissive phrase 'What part of no don't you understand' in biblical language
jokes.explanation: Clever modernization of biblical speech pattern - very quotable

jokes.text: You can't give up hope just because it's hopeless. You gotta hope even more and cover your ears and go, 'Blah, blah!'
jokes.setup: Leela says the search is hopeless
jokes.punchline: Fry's completely irrational but earnest philosophy of denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry naivety - childish solution to existential problem delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Now that's one Bible that doesn't disappoint.
jokes.setup: Bender finds what appears to be a Bible or holy book
jokes.punchline: Implies regular Bible is disappointing, this one isn't
jokes.explanation: Good Bender irreverence - casual dismissal of actual religious texts

jokes.text: Convert them? To radioactive vapor!
jokes.setup: Talk of converting unbelievers
jokes.punchline: Religious conversion becomes literal nuclear conversion to vapor
jokes.explanation: Dark wordplay on religious conversion using nuclear terminology

jokes.text: Look, Daddy, I'm hugging God.
jokes.setup: Nuclear explosion beginning to vaporize tiny civilization
jokes.punchline: Child innocently describes being destroyed as 'hugging God'
jokes.explanation: Darkly effective - innocent child's perspective on apocalyptic destruction

jokes.text: Hello? Is anyone still alive? How about in the porno theater? Don't be embarrassed.
jokes.setup: Bender checking for survivors after nuclear war
jokes.punchline: Assumes porn theater patrons are just hiding from shame, not dead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about post-apocalyptic priorities and social embarrassment

jokes.text: Who would have known playing God could have such terrible consequences?
jokes.setup: Bender surveys the destruction his godhood caused
jokes.punchline: Completely obvious lesson delivered as genuine surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender obliviousness - treats obvious moral lesson as shocking revelation

jokes.text: None of that 'huddled for warmth' crap.
jokes.setup: Fry worried about dying of exhaustion
jokes.punchline: Vanity about death pose more important than survival instincts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's concern with dignity even in death

jokes.text: Please stand to one side to let faster pilgrims by.
jokes.setup: Welcoming visitors to sacred monastery
jokes.punchline: Treating spiritual pilgrimage like a traffic jam
jokes.explanation: Funny bureaucratization of spiritual journey - pilgrimage as rush hour

jokes.text: But I only know enough binary to ask for the bathroom. Speak English?
jokes.setup: Trying to communicate with galaxy in binary
jokes.punchline: Bender's limited binary vocabulary restricted to bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Great robot joke - even Bender's programming has practical limitations

jokes.text: What are you, a galactic computer? Possible. I am user-Friendly, my good chum.
jokes.setup: Bender tries to identify mysterious cosmic being
jokes.punchline: Entity uses computer terminology 'user-friendly' literally
jokes.explanation: Mild computer pun with godlike entity speaking in tech support language

jokes.text: There he is! No. No, wait. No.
jokes.setup: Monk claims to spot God through telescope after 700 years
jokes.punchline: Immediately recants multiple times
jokes.explanation: Simple running gag - monk's false alarms after centuries of searching

jokes.text: Like a giant karaoke machine? Not really. Want to see our giant karaoke machine? Not really.
jokes.setup: Monk compares telescope to karaoke machine
jokes.punchline: Monastery actually has giant karaoke machine, neither party interested
jokes.explanation: Absurd monastery amenities and mutual lack of interest creates nice beat

jokes.text: He speaks out of love for his friend. Perhaps that love is God.
jokes.setup: Fry arguing passionately for telescope access
jokes.punchline: Monk suggests they've found God without needing the telescope
jokes.explanation: Nice thematic joke that undercuts the entire telescope quest with simple wisdom

jokes.text: Actually, we only practice martial arts as meditation. We are strictly nonviolent.
jokes.setup: Fry threatens to fight the martial arts monks
jokes.punchline: Monks reveal their fighting skills are purely spiritual
jokes.explanation: Standard subversion of martial arts monk trope - all show, no combat

jokes.text: Do you know what I'll do before I do it? Yes. What if I do something different? Then I don't know that. Cool, cool.
jokes.setup: Testing God's omniscience
jokes.punchline: God's knowledge limited by free will paradox, Bender satisfied by logic
jokes.explanation: Clever theological paradox resolved with Bender's casual acceptance

jokes.text: You must use a light touch, like a safecracker or a pickpocket.
jokes.setup: God explaining proper divine intervention technique
jokes.punchline: Compares godhood to criminal skills
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy - divine wisdom compared to criminal expertise, perfectly unexpected

jokes.text: Or a guy who burns a bar for insurance money! Yes, if you make it look like an electrical thing.
jokes.setup: God mentions light touch like safecracker or pickpocket
jokes.punchline: Bender adds insurance fraud example, God approves technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender criminal mindset meeting divine wisdom - God's practical advice on crime

jokes.text: When you do things right, people won't be sure you've done anything at all.
jokes.setup: God explaining divine intervention philosophy
jokes.punchline: Core wisdom about effective action being invisible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical insight that became widely quoted - perfect economy and universal truth

jokes.text: Has anyone out there seen Bender? No! Quit asking!
jokes.setup: Fry continuing his obsessive search
jokes.punchline: Monks' exasperated response to repeated question
jokes.explanation: Standard running gag payoff - Fry's persistence becoming annoying

jokes.text: I know he was evil, and on more than one occasion... He actually stole my blood.
jokes.setup: Fry acknowledging Bender's flaws
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of literal blood theft as relationship quirk
jokes.explanation: Perfect friendship dynamic - treating vampiric behavior as minor character flaw

jokes.text: This is, by a wide margin, the least likely thing ever to happen.
jokes.setup: Bender miraculously returns from space
jokes.punchline: Leela's statistical assessment of the impossible reunion
jokes.explanation: Leela's rational reaction to plot convenience - acknowledging the miraculous

jokes.text: Fat chance! You can't count on God for jack! He pretty much told me so!
jokes.setup: Suggestion that God will help the trapped monks
jokes.punchline: Bender's nihilistic interpretation of God's light touch philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender misinterpretation of divine wisdom as excuse for cynicism

jokes.text: When you do things right, people won't be sure you've done anything at all.
jokes.setup: Final scene showing God's subtle intervention
jokes.punchline: Repetition of core philosophy as episode's thesis statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect structural callback that transforms earlier line into profound episode thesis
